ddls:
CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the user */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user's country */,
  "education_level_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user's education level */,
  "number_of_languages_spoken" INTEGER /* Number of languages the user speaks */,
  "gender_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user's gender */,
  "satisfaction_rate" INTEGER /* User's satisfaction rate on a scale of 1-10 */,
  "marital_status_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user's marital status */,
  "number_of_children" INTEGER /* Number of children the user has */,
  "registration_date" TEXT /* Date when the user registered */,
  "last_activity_date" TEXT /* Date of the user's last activity */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the user is currently active */,
  "preferred_language_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user's preferred language */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the user */,
  "annual_income" INTEGER /* Annual income of the user */,
  "user_segment_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user's primary segment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_education_level_id FOREIGN KEY ("education_level_id") REFERENCES education_levels ("education_level_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_gender_id FOREIGN KEY ("gender_id") REFERENCES genders ("gender_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_preferred_language_id FOREIGN KEY ("preferred_language_id") REFERENCES languages_spoken ("language_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_user_segment_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_segment_id") REFERENCES user_segments ("segment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_marital_status_id FOREIGN KEY ("marital_status_id") REFERENCES marital_statuses ("marital_status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region where the country is located */,
  "currency" TEXT /* Currency used in the country */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "education_levels" (
  "education_level_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each education level */,
  "education_level_name" TEXT /* Name of the education level */,
  "degree_name" TEXT /* Name of the degree (if applicable) */,
  "field_of_study" TEXT /* Field of study (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("education_level_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genders" (
  "gender_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each gender */,
  "gender_name" TEXT /* Name of the gender */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("gender_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "marital_statuses" (
  "marital_status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each marital status */,
  "marital_status_name" TEXT /* Name of the marital status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("marital_status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "languages_spoken" (
  "language_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each language */,
  "language_name" TEXT /* Name of the language */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("language_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_languages" (
  "user_language_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user-language pair */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user */,
  "language_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the language */,
  "proficiency_level" TEXT /* Proficiency level in the language (e.g., Beginner, Intermediate, Advanced) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_language_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_languages_language_id FOREIGN KEY ("language_id") REFERENCES languages_spoken ("language_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_languages_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_feedback" (
  "feedback_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each feedback entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who provided feedback */,
  "feedback_date" TEXT /* Date when the feedback was provided */,
  "feedback_text" TEXT /* Text of the feedback */,
  "feedback_rating" INTEGER /* Rating given by the user along with the feedback (1-5 scale) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feedback_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_feedback_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_satisfaction_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each satisfaction rate record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user */,
  "satisfaction_rate" INTEGER /* User's satisfaction rate on a scale of 1-10 */,
  "record_date" TEXT /* Date when the satisfaction rate was recorded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_satisfaction_history_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_activities" (
  "activity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user activity */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user */,
  "activity_type" TEXT /* Type of activity (e.g., login, purchase, view) */,
  "activity_date" TEXT /* Date when the activity occurred */,
  "activity_details" TEXT /* Details of the activity */,
  "activity_location" TEXT /* Location where the activity occurred */,
  "activity_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the activity in minutes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("activity_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_activities_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_segments" (
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user segment */,
  "segment_name" TEXT /* Name of the user segment */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the user segment */,
  "segment_criteria" TEXT /* Criteria used to define the segment */,
  "segment_size" INTEGER /* Number of users in the segment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("segment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_segment_mapping" (
  "mapping_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user-segment mapping */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user */,
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the segment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mapping_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_segment_mapping_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_segment_mapping_segment_id FOREIGN KEY ("segment_id") REFERENCES user_segments ("segment_id")
)
